text: Replacing 2 easy runs per week with outdoor elliptical bike cross-training improved functional movement screen scores with a large effect size in high school cross-country runners over 4 weeks.
Outdoor elliptical bike cross-training was the only modality that improved running economy when substituted for 2 easy runs per week over 4 weeks.
Cycling cross-training substituted for 2 easy runs per week produced large improvements in 3,000-m performance over 4 weeks, comparable to outdoor elliptical bike training.

All cross-training modalities (cycling, indoor elliptical, outdoor elliptical bike) and running-only training improved 3,000-m performance over 4 weeks, but only cycling and outdoor elliptical bike showed large effect sizes.
Running-only, cycling, and outdoor elliptical bike training all improved maximal hip extensor strength, while indoor elliptical training did not.
Outdoor cycling and outdoor elliptical bike are the most effective cross-training modalities for early-season training to improve running performance when replacing easy runs.
